explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A press release indicates a commitment to AI governance, mentioning AI and collaboration to advance AI-enabled optimization. It describes concrete actions related to operational execution, such as "design and test" and "deployments."

relevant_sources.summary: This press release, "NOV and AIQ AI-Enabled Drilling," provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The document indicates a commitment to AI governance by mentioning AI and collaboration to advance AI-enabled optimization. Furthermore, it demonstrates operational execution through descriptions of concrete actions like "design and test" and "deployments."
relevant_sources.title: NOV and AIQ AI-Enabled Drilling

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Drawing from 9 publicly available sources, NOV's published materials address 1 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Governance is addressed at the policy level, with disclosures indicating a commitment to AI governance and mentioning AI and collaboration to advance AI-enabled optimization. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
